name: always_unfold_intensional_bridge
reason: always_unfold is external_body bridging the intensional gap between the forall inside always's closure and a bare forall in the ensures — this unverified axiom could be unsound if Verus's SMT encoding ever distinguishes these closures

name: always_propagate_suffix_composition
reason: chaining always_propagate_forwards with always_unfold gives access to p on doubly-nested suffixes ex.suffix(i).suffix(j), which is intensionally distinct from ex.suffix(i+j) — the axioms implicitly collapse this distinction without proof

name: always_double_unfold_to_bare
reason: always_double composes with double-unfolding to yield p on ex.suffix(i).suffix(j) — if always(always(p)) can be freely unfolded twice, the external_body axioms together provide arbitrary suffix composition without proving suffix(i).suffix(j) == suffix(i+j)

name: always_propagate_unbounded
reason: the two external_body axioms together yield a universally quantified double-suffix property — this unbounded composition amplifies trust surface since suffix nesting never reduces to a single offset without an explicit composition lemma

name: always_propagate_zero_redundant
reason: always_propagate_forwards at i=0 asserts always(p) holds on ex.suffix(0), but ex.suffix(0) wraps nat_to_state in a new closure |i| f(i+0) which is intensionally distinct from ex — the axiom silently equates these without proof
